tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to apply for a meaningful scholarship covering next term's costs, though I'm also tempted by immediate fun. Given my low energy and sleep debt, I need to decide if focusing on this solo task now is better than resting.
Starting this scholarship application now converts friction into slack, lowering Thursday stress without sacrificing my fun plans later.

I've made some progress on the scholarship application, but I still have significant work left before my deadline. With half my free block gone and high stress, I need to decide if I should continue or take a break.
I advanced my scholarship application by 10%, but letting the immediate fun opportunity pass increases my regret while I remain fatigued and stressed.
I've completed 1.5 hours of the scholarship work, increasing progress to 60% with two days left. I still have significant effort remaining before my deadline.
I've completed 1.5 hours of the scholarship work, increasing progress to 66% with two days left before the deadline.
I am at 72% progress on my scholarship essay with only three days left, yet my energy is zero and stress remains high.
I've completed 1.5 hours of scholarship work, raising progress to 78% with two days left, but my energy is zero and stress remains high.
I advanced my scholarship progress to 84% despite zero energy and high stress, confirming I am burning out while racing against a tight deadline.
I advanced my scholarship progress to 89% despite zero energy and high stress, confirming I am burning out while racing against a tight deadline.
I pushed my scholarship progress to 95% despite zero energy and high stress, confirming I am burning out while racing against a tight deadline.
I finished the scholarship application despite having zero energy and significant sleep debt, confirming I am burning out while racing against a tight deadline.

goals.rationale: This task has no hard deadline until Monday, allowing it to be scheduled after the high-priority scholarship application is finished or as a brief break if energy permits.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
